mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p-value < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration triggers recession fears that depress corporate earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.02 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous peaks in retail sales and housing starts indicate saturated consumer demand that inevitably rolls over, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns as growth expectations adjust downward.
failure_condition: Joint peaks in RSAFS and HOUST fail to precede negative equity returns, or the interaction term shows zero marginal predictive power in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production confirms robust manufacturing output that supports corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation combined with restrictive monetary policy creates stagflationary pressure that compresses profit margins and forces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of high CPIAUCSL and high FEDFUNDS yields positive forward returns in > 60% of cases, invalidating the stagflation penalty mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls expand household income and consumer spending capacity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with positive 20-day equity returns, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP expansion exceeding 11000.0 billion confirms macroeconomic momentum that lifts corporate earnings forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP levels above 11000.0 are followed by negative equity returns in > 50% of observations, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp reduction in the federal funds rate injects liquidity into financial markets and lowers discount rates, triggering immediate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rate cuts of 0.25% or more fail to generate positive 5-day returns, or the market prices in the cut beforehand resulting in flat or negative forward performance.
